---
title: "NguyenKhang_33326460_Assignment3"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-18"
---
output:
  html_document: default
  pdf_document: default
---
#### GENERATIVE AI DECLERATION:
In this assignment, I used Gemini Chatbot to brainstorm, improve writing and clarity, explain the coding, concepts, and suggest how to approach the implementation.
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  eval = TRUE,
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,   
  warning = FALSE    
)
```
#### Import data set and load library

```{r}
# Load tidyverse
library(tidyverse)
```
```{r message = FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# You can download this file in this drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/13RS_DPyM67KVr4nBs1xzBIlzE80KHSv-?usp=sharing 
# import the data set
mosquito_data <-read_csv('mosquito_borne_disease_climate_dataset.csv')
```

```{r}
# Isolate ecological indicators and evaluate outbreak relationships
ecological_synergy <- mosquito_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(Humidity_rate_percent), !is.na(Stagnant_water_area_m2), !is.na(Mosquito_Density_per_Ha))

# Generate a continuous scatter visualization color-scaled by case volumes
ggplot(ecological_synergy, aes(x = Humidity_rate_percent, y = Stagnant_water_area_m2, color = Mosquito_Density_per_Ha)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, size = 2) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "inferno") +
  labs(
    title = "Analysis 1: Mosqutio density Distribution across Humidity & Stagnant Water",
    x = "Atmospheric Humidity Rate (%)",
    y = "Stagnant Water Surface Area (sq meters)",
    color = "Mosqutio density"
  ) +
  theme_classic()


```
```{r}
# Proving that some place that has low mosquito density despite has high stagnant water and humidity rate.
high_risk_environments <- mosquito_data %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(Humidity_rate_percent), 
    !is.na(Stagnant_water_area_m2), 
    !is.na(Pesticide_spray_count_per_month), 
    !is.na(Mosquito_Density_per_Ha)
  ) %>%
  # Filter for records exceeding the median for both humidity and stagnant water
  filter(
    Humidity_rate_percent > median(Humidity_rate_percent, na.rm = TRUE),
    Stagnant_water_area_m2 > median(Stagnant_water_area_m2, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

# Plot pesticide applications against the resulting vector density in these hot spots
ggplot(high_risk_environments, aes(x = Pesticide_spray_count_per_month, y = Mosquito_Density_per_Ha)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6, aes(size = Stagnant_water_area_m2, color = Humidity_rate_percent)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "blue", linetype = "solid", linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "mako") +
  labs(
    title = "Analysis 2: Pesticide Control Efficacy Within Mosquito density rate",
    x = "Pesticide Spray Count (per Month)",
    y = "Resulting Mosquito Density (per Hectare)",
    color = "Humidity Rate (%)",
    size = "Stagnant Water (m²)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
# Filter out missing records to ensure statistical accuracy
susceptibility_trends <- mosquito_data %>%
  filter(Cases_count<=2000000,!is.na(Susceptible_population_rate_percent), !is.na(Cases_count))

# Generate a scatter plot with an advanced non-linear smoothed trendline
ggplot(susceptibility_trends, aes(x = Susceptible_population_rate_percent, y = Cases_count)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, aes(color = Country)) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", color = "darkred", linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(scale = 1e-6, suffix = "M")) +
  labs(
    title = "Analysis 3: Impact of Population Susceptibility on Case Counts",
    x = "Susceptible Population Rate (%)",
    y = "Total Confirmed Clinical Cases (Millions case)",
    color = "Country"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


```
```{r}
#Healthcare Infrastructure Vulnerability Matrix

# Calculate a vulnerability index comparing cases to available hospital infrastructure
infrastructure_vulnerability <- mosquito_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(Hospital_beds), !is.na(Cases_count), !is.na(Health_funding_AUD)) %>%
  group_by(Country, Province) %>%
  summarise(
    Average_cases = mean(Cases_count),
    Average_Bed_Capacity = mean(Hospital_beds),
    Bed_Deficit_Ratio = Average_cases / (Average_Bed_Capacity + 1), # Insulates against division by zero
    .groups = 'drop'
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Bed_Deficit_Ratio))

# Print a structured markdown table summarizing the top 5 most vulnerable regions
infrastructure_vulnerability %>% 
  slice_max(order_by = Bed_Deficit_Ratio, n = 10)

```

```{r}
# Segment records into categorical vaccination capacity tiers
# Clean missing parameters and trace immunization trends
vaccination_suppression <- mosquito_data %>%
  filter(Cases_count<=500000,!is.na(Vaccination_rate_percent), !is.na(Cases_count))

# Visualize the negative correlation to identify safety thresholds
ggplot(vaccination_suppression, aes(x = Vaccination_rate_percent, y = Cases_count)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, color = "cadetblue") +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "forestgreen", linewidth = 1.2, se = TRUE) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::label_number(scale = 1e-5, suffix = "M")) +
  labs(
    title = "Analysis 4: Evaluating Outbreak Suppression via Regional Vaccination Coverage",
    x = "Community Vaccination Rate (%)",
    y = "Total Confirmed Clinical Cases (hundread thoundsands)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()
```
